name: Topaz Authorization API
what_it_does: Open-source authorization service combining OPA (Open Policy Agent) policy evaluation with a Zanzibar-style relationship graph — providing fine-grained authorization for applications with both attribute-based (ABAC) and relationship-based (ReBAC) access control via gRPC/REST API.
use_cases: Fine-grained authorization checks (can user X perform action Y on resource Z?) via decision API
Managing authorization relationships (owner, editor, viewer) in a graph store via Directory API
Combining OPA policy logic with relationship graph for hybrid ABAC+ReBAC authorization
Self-hosted alternative to Aserto cloud with full data control for sensitive environments
Evaluating authorization decisions at the edge with low-latency local policy engine
not_for: Authentication (Topaz handles authorization only — pair with Keycloak or Ory Kratos for authn)
Teams without engineering capacity to maintain self-hosted policy infrastructure
Simple role-based access control without relationship graphs (use Permit.io or Cerbos for simpler setups)
best_when: A team needs fine-grained authorization combining relationship graphs (Zanzibar-style) with policy logic (OPA) in a self-hosted, high-performance local service.
avoid_when: You need managed authorization-as-a-service without operational overhead — use Permit.io, Cerbos Cloud, or SpiceDB Cloud instead.

auth.notes: API key for REST access. mTLS for gRPC connections. Self-hosted — auth model is operator-configured. No fine-grained scopes at API level. Policy defines authorization; API auth is infrastructure-level.

pricing.paid_tiers: Aserto Cloud (managed Topaz) has paid tiers; self-hosted is free
pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 open source. Built by Aserto team as open-source version of their cloud product. Self-hosted deployment via Docker or binary. Strong OPA ecosystem integration. Good for teams needing full data control with fine-grained authorization.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: gRPC primary interface with REST gateway. Authorizer API for decisions; Directory API for relationship management. Multi-language SDKs. OPA policy as code for authorization logic. Decision API returns structured allow/deny with reason.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Authorization checks are fully idempotent reads. Directory object/relation writes are idempotent. Policy evaluation deterministic for same inputs.
known_agent_gotchas: OPA policy authoring required — Rego language learning curve
gRPC primary interface — REST gateway available but gRPC is more ergonomic
Self-hosted only — no managed option without moving to Aserto Cloud
Directory (relationship store) and Authorizer (policy engine) are separate services

security_score_components.security_notes: Apache 2.0 open source — fully auditable Go codebase. mTLS for gRPC. Self-hosted gives full data control. OPA policy as code — auditable authorization logic. No formal certifications (self-hosted). Policy evaluation in memory — no external calls.
